decomposition_notes: Four goals, one per seam in the doc's '## Decomposition seams' section, per the explicit instruction that this initiative ships as multiple independent goals scheduled one at a time. Grounding verified against the codebase: the data layer is the read-only Kanban.Agents context (lib/kanban/agents.ex) deriving Agent/Event structs (lib/kanban/agents/agent.ex, event.ex) from Kanban.Tasks.Task; the web layer is KanbanWeb.AgentsLive (lib/kanban_web/live/agents_live.ex) composing AgentsHeader, AgentRosterCard, and AgentActivityFeed components, with AgentsPresence for the live indicator. Owner linkage data already exists: Task has belongs_to :created_by/:completed_by (Accounts.User) alongside the created_by_agent/completed_by_agent string fields, so the Owner-linkage goal needs no schema change (Constraint: read-only, no schema changes). Each goal spans the data->context->web layers for its own slice but those tasks are code-coupled within the slice (one Agent/Event field, one component) so they stay together. CLAIM ORDER across goals reflects doc-stated dependencies: G1 (Owner linkage) is foundational for 'show the person not the agent' and should be claimed first; G2 (Fleet health) explicitly depends on first fixing display bugs D83/D84/D85, so its first tasks are those defect fixes; G3 (PM trends) and G4 (Developer drill-down) are independent of each other and of G2, and may be claimed in any order after G1. No cross-goal array-index dependencies are encoded (the batch API cannot express them); within each goal tasks use array-index dependencies. Verification commands are limited to `mix test <path>` against existing test files/conventions and manual curl-of-localhost:4000 / browser checks, since no other test command is justified by the doc or codebase. EDIT (post-decomposition): the three 'Fix D83/D84/D85' tasks were removed from the fleet-health goal because those defects already exist as standalone Stride tasks (D83, D84, D85); claim and complete those first, then the fleet-health rollup tasks. The goal title was narrowed accordingly and the rollup tasks no longer carry an index dependency on the removed fixes.

goals.title: Surface the human owner behind each agent across the /agents roster and feed
goals.why: The /agents view identifies work only by agent model name (e.g. 'Claude Opus 4.8') and never ties it to the human owner/operator behind it. This is the foundational slice for the initiative's core goal of showing the person, not just the agent. The linkage data already exists on Task (belongs_to :created_by / :completed_by -> Accounts.User) but is not derived or displayed.
goals.what: Extend the read-only Kanban.Agents context to derive a human owner/operator for each agent and event from the existing Task associations, then surface that owner alongside the agent model name on the roster cards and in the activity feed. No schema or task-lifecycle changes.
goals.description: Map each derived agent to the human who owns/operates it using the Task.created_by / Task.completed_by User associations that already exist, and render the person next to the agent model name on the roster and in the feed. Read-only over existing data; must work in light and dark mode and respect existing board scope.
goals.acceptance_criteria:
Each agent in the roster shows the human owner/operator (when one can be derived) alongside the agent model name
Activity feed events show the owner alongside the actor agent name
Owner derivation reuses existing Task->User associations; no schema, migration, or task-lifecycle changes are introduced
When no owner can be derived, the UI degrades gracefully (agent name only, no broken layout)
Board scope filtering and existing PubSub live updates continue to work
Roster and feed render correctly in both light and dark mode
pitfalls: Do not add a schema, migration, or new Ecto field — owner linkage must come from existing Task associations only
Do not put Ecto queries in the LiveView or components — owner derivation belongs in Kanban.Agents
Do not break the existing board-scope access filtering when joining the owner association

tasks.title: Add owner field to the derived Agent struct in Kanban.Agents.Agent
tasks.description: Add an :owner field to the Kanban.Agents.Agent struct and @type to carry the derived human owner/operator (e.g. %{id, name/email} or nil) alongside the existing agent model :name. Default to nil so existing callers keep working.
tasks.why: The roster card needs a place to read the person from; the struct currently only carries the agent model name.
tasks.what: Add :owner to defstruct (defaulting nil) and to the @type t spec in agent.ex.
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Mirror the existing field + @type style already in agent.ex (status, current_task)
Keep the owner value a plain map or nil, not an Ecto schema struct
pitfalls: Do not make owner required — many agents may have no derivable owner

verification_steps.expected_result: All existing agents context tests still pass

unit_tests: Agent struct defaults owner to nil

tasks.title: Derive agent owner from Task created_by/completed_by in Kanban.Agents.list_agents
tasks.description: In Kanban.Agents, populate the new Agent :owner by resolving the human User behind each derived agent from the existing Task associations (created_by / completed_by). Preload or join the User association inside the context's task fetch so no query leaks into the web layer. Respect the existing :scope board filtering.
tasks.why: The struct field added in the prior task must be filled from real data; this is where the person-behind-the-agent linkage is computed.
tasks.what: Resolve the owner for each distinct agent name from the visible Task set's created_by/completed_by User associations and assign it to Agent.owner.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
list_agents returns agents with :owner populated when a User can be derived from created_by/completed_by
:owner is nil when no User can be derived
The User association is loaded inside the context (preload/join), not in the LiveView
Existing :scope board filtering is preserved
No schema or migration changes
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Follow the existing fetch_tasks/build_agent pattern in agents.ex
Keep all Ecto.Query usage inside the context module
pitfalls: Avoid an N+1 query when resolving owners — preload or join in the single task fetch
Do not break board-scope filtering when adding the association

key_files.note: Resolve and assign Agent.owner from Task associations

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass including new owner-derivation cases

unit_tests: Agent owner derived from created_by user
Agent owner derived from completed_by user when created_by absent
Owner nil when no user association

tasks.title: Render owner alongside agent name on the roster card
tasks.description: Update AgentRosterCard to display the derived owner (person) alongside the agent model name. When owner is nil, show the agent name alone without layout breakage. Use existing stride-screen design tokens; verify both light and dark mode.
tasks.why: Ops and developers need to see who owns each agent at a glance on the roster.
tasks.what: Add an owner line/label to the roster card markup, reading agent.owner.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Roster card shows the owner alongside the agent model name when present
Card renders cleanly with no owner (agent name only)
Uses existing design tokens (var(--ink-*), etc.), no hardcoded colors
Readable in light and dark mode
UI text is gettext-wrapped
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Follow existing markup and token usage in agent_roster_card.ex
Wrap visible strings in gettext and run mix gettext.extract --merge
pitfalls: Do not hardcode Tailwind colors — use the existing design-system tokens
Do not break the selected-state styling already on the card

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass including owner rendering

verification_steps.step_text: Open /agents at localhost:4000 and confirm the owner shows next to the agent name on roster cards in both light and dark mode
verification_steps.expected_result: Owner visible alongside agent name, no layout breakage, readable in both modes

unit_tests: Card renders agent name only when owner nil
manual_tests: Visual check in light and dark mode

tasks.title: Add owner to derived Event and surface it in the activity feed
tasks.description: Add an :owner field to Kanban.Agents.Event, populate it in Kanban.Agents.recent_activity from the same Task->User associations, and render the owner alongside the actor agent name in AgentActivityFeed. Degrade gracefully when nil.
tasks.why: The feed currently shows only the actor agent name; ops/devs need to see the person behind each event too, consistently with the roster.
tasks.what: Extend Event struct + recent_activity derivation + feed markup to carry and display the owner.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Event struct has an :owner field (nil default) with updated @type
recent_activity populates owner from the same Task->User associations used for the roster
Activity feed renders owner alongside the actor agent name when present
Feed degrades gracefully when owner is nil
Uses existing tokens; readable in light and dark mode; UI text is gettext-wrapped
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Mirror the Agent owner derivation from the earlier task for consistency
Follow existing event-row markup in agent_activity_feed.ex
pitfalls: Avoid N+1 — reuse the same preloaded/joined association strategy as the roster
Do not change the existing kind/actor filtering behavior in the feed

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass including Event owner derivation

verification_steps.step_text: Open /agents at localhost:4000 and confirm the feed shows the owner alongside the actor agent name
verification_steps.expected_result: Owner visible in feed rows where derivable, no breakage when absent

unit_tests: Event owner nil when no user
manual_tests: Feed visual check in both modes

goals.title: Add a fleet-health rollup to /agents
goals.why: Ops cannot answer 'is the fleet healthy right now?' from the current page, and stuck/idle agents go unnoticed (a lagging metric is dropping stuck-agent detection time from a day to under an hour). The doc states this rollup depends on first correcting the known display bugs D83 (status mislabeling), D84 (sort order), and D85 (stat overlap) so the underlying data can be trusted.
goals.what: Fix the three known display bugs, then add a fleet-health rollup at the top of /agents that classifies agents as working / waiting / stuck / idle with counts and visual emphasis on stuck and idle agents. Read-only over existing derived data.
goals.description: Correct the trust-blocking display bugs, then surface a health rollup so ops sees who is working/waiting/stuck/idle at a glance, with counts and emphasis on stuck and idle agents. Live PubSub updates and board scope must keep working in both light and dark mode.
goals.acceptance_criteria:
D83 status mislabeling fixed — agent status labels are correct
D84 sort order fixed — roster/feed ordering is correct and stable
D85 stat overlap fixed — header/roster stats no longer double-count or overlap
A fleet-health rollup shows counts of working / waiting / stuck / idle agents
Stuck and idle agents are visually emphasized
Rollup updates live via the existing PubSub flow and respects board scope
Works in light and dark mode
pitfalls: Do not introduce schema or task-lifecycle changes — health classification is derived only
Do not regress the existing live PubSub refresh/debounce behavior
Do not put status-classification logic in the LiveView — keep it in Kanban.Agents

tasks.title: Add stuck-agent classification to Kanban.Agents
tasks.description: Extend agent status derivation so an agent that has stalled or is sitting in review past a reasonable threshold is classified as stuck (in addition to working/waiting/idle), purely from existing Task timestamps (claimed_at, reviewed_at, review_status, completed_at). This powers the rollup's emphasis on stuck agents and the stuck-agent-detection-time metric. (Prerequisite: standalone defects D83, D84, and D85 must be fixed first; they are tracked separately and are not part of this goal.)
tasks.why: The doc's lagging metric is reducing stuck-agent detection time; the page cannot emphasize stuck agents without first deriving stuck-ness.
tasks.what: Add a stuck classification (e.g. extend Agent.status or a derived flag) computed from existing timestamps, with a documented threshold.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Agents that have stalled or are sitting in review past a documented threshold are classified as stuck
Classification uses only existing Task fields (no schema change)
Threshold is a named module attribute, documented in the moduledoc
Unit tests cover stuck vs working/waiting/idle boundaries
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Follow the existing status derivation in build_agent (fixed by D83 first)
Use a named @attribute for the threshold like other timing constants
pitfalls: Do not require a schema field for stuck-ness — derive it from timestamps
Build on the D83 fix; do not reintroduce mislabeling

key_files.note: Add stuck to status or a stuck flag + @type

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass including stuck-classification cases

unit_tests: Agent stalled past threshold is stuck
Agent in review past threshold is stuck
Agent just below threshold is not stuck

tasks.title: Add fleet-health rollup counts to Kanban.Agents
tasks.description: Add a context function (e.g. Kanban.Agents.fleet_health/1) that returns counts of agents by status (working / waiting / stuck / idle) for the scoped, visible agent set, reusing the corrected status derivation. Respects the :scope option.
tasks.why: The rollup component needs a single trustworthy data source for the health counts; aggregation belongs in the context, not the LiveView.
tasks.what: Add a scoped fleet_health aggregation returning per-status counts.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
fleet_health returns counts for working, waiting, stuck, and idle for the scoped agent set
Reuses the corrected/extended status derivation (D83 + stuck)
Respects the :scope board filtering
Unit tests cover the counts including the empty case
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Follow the opts/:scope convention used by list_agents/header_stats
Keep the function read-only
pitfalls: Do not recompute status differently from list_agents — share the derivation to avoid drift

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass including fleet_health counts

unit_tests: Empty agent set returns all zeros

tasks.title: Render the fleet-health rollup on /agents with emphasis on stuck and idle
tasks.description: Add a fleet-health rollup to the top of the /agents page (in or beside AgentsHeader) showing working / waiting / stuck / idle counts, with visual emphasis on stuck and idle. Wire it in AgentsLive's load_agents_data via the new fleet_health function so it updates on the existing PubSub refresh. Use existing tokens; verify both modes.
tasks.why: Ops needs the health answer at a glance; this is the user-facing payoff of the goal and supports faster stuck-agent detection.
tasks.what: Add the rollup component, assign fleet_health in the LiveView, and emphasize stuck/idle.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Rollup shows working / waiting / stuck / idle counts at the top of /agents
Stuck and idle agents are visually emphasized
LiveView assigns fleet_health in load_agents_data so it refreshes on the existing PubSub debounce
Uses existing design tokens; readable in light and dark mode; UI text gettext-wrapped
Board scope respected (counts reflect the scoped agent set)
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Follow the existing AgentsHeader composition and load_agents_data assign pattern
Wrap strings in gettext and run mix gettext.extract --merge
pitfalls: Do not query in the LiveView — read from Kanban.Agents.fleet_health
Do not regress the existing refresh debounce / presence behavior
Do not hardcode colors for the emphasis styling

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass including the rollup rendering

verification_steps.step_text: Open /agents at localhost:4000 and confirm the health rollup shows working/waiting/stuck/idle counts with stuck/idle emphasized, in both light and dark mode
verification_steps.expected_result: Rollup visible and correct, updates live, readable in both modes

unit_tests: Rollup renders the four status counts
integration_tests: LiveView re-renders rollup on agent_event broadcast

goals.title: Add throughput and quality trend views for product managers on /agents
goals.why: Product managers leave /agents without throughput, success-rate, and cycle-time trends; the page should let PMs see throughput and quality trends so they go there first and trust it. Open question in the doc: which trends matter most — start with throughput, success rate, and cycle time, which are all derivable from existing Task data.
goals.what: Add aggregate and time-series derivations (throughput, success rate, cycle time) to the read-only Kanban.Agents context and surface them on /agents for the PM persona, within the existing LiveView and design system. No schema or workflow changes.
goals.description: Derive throughput (completions over time), success rate (completed vs failed/abandoned), and cycle time (existing Event.cycle_time_minutes / claim-to-complete) as aggregates and simple time-series, and present them in a PM-focused section of /agents. Read-only; respects scope; works in both modes. Not a BI tool — no report builder, no exportable charts (non-goal).
goals.acceptance_criteria:
Throughput, success rate, and cycle-time trends are derived from existing Task data with no schema changes
Trends are presented in a PM-focused section of /agents within the existing design system
Section respects board scope and updates with the existing live refresh
Readable in light and dark mode
Scope stays within the page — no report builder, dashboards-as-code, or exportable charts
pitfalls: Do not build a generic BI/report-builder — non-goal
Do not add exportable charts — non-goal
Do not put aggregation queries in the LiveView — keep them in Kanban.Agents
Do not introduce schema or workflow changes

tasks.title: Derive throughput and success-rate aggregates in Kanban.Agents
tasks.description: Add a scoped context function returning fleet throughput (completion counts over a window) and overall success rate, derived from existing Task fields (completed_at, review_status, status). Respect the :scope option. Read-only.
tasks.why: PMs need throughput and quality numbers; aggregation must live in the context.
tasks.what: Add a throughput + success-rate aggregate function to Kanban.Agents.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Function returns throughput counts over a defined window and an overall success rate
Derived only from existing Task fields; no schema change
Respects :scope
Unit tests cover happy path and empty data
tasks.patterns_to_follow: Follow the opts/:scope and fetch_tasks conventions in agents.ex
pitfalls: Do not double-count completions already fixed under D85 conventions
Avoid N+1 — aggregate from the single task fetch

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass including throughput/success-rate cases

tasks.title: Derive cycle-time and a throughput time-series in Kanban.Agents
tasks.description: Add a scoped context function returning a simple time-series of throughput (e.g. per day over a window) and an aggregate cycle-time metric, reusing the existing Event.cycle_time_minutes / claim-to-complete derivation. Read-only, respects :scope.
tasks.why: Trends (not just point values) are what PMs track; cycle time is one of the three trends called out.
tasks.what: Add a time-series throughput + cycle-time aggregate to Kanban.Agents.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Function returns a per-bucket throughput series over a window and an aggregate cycle-time metric
Reuses existing cycle_time derivation; no schema change
Respects :scope
Unit tests cover bucketing and empty windows
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Reuse the existing cycle_time_minutes computation feeding Event
Follow the opts/:scope convention
pitfalls: Do not invent a new cycle-time definition — reuse the existing one
Keep bucketing deterministic across time zones

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass including time-series and cycle-time cases

tasks.title: Add a PM trends component and render it on /agents
tasks.description: Create a PM-focused trends component showing throughput (with its time-series), success rate, and cycle time, and render it in a section of /agents. Wire the new aggregates into AgentsLive.load_agents_data so they refresh on the existing PubSub debounce. Use existing tokens; verify both modes; gettext all text.
tasks.why: This is the PM-facing payoff: a place on /agents where PMs see throughput and quality trends.
tasks.what: Add the trends component, assign the aggregates in the LiveView, and render the PM section.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
A PM trends section on /agents shows throughput (incl. time-series), success rate, and cycle time
Aggregates assigned in load_agents_data and refresh on the existing PubSub flow
Uses existing design tokens; readable in light and dark mode; text gettext-wrapped
Board scope respected
No exportable charts / report builder
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Follow the existing component + load_agents_data assign pattern in agents_live.ex
Keep the time-series rendering simple (no charting library); use the existing design system
Run mix gettext.extract --merge after adding text
pitfalls: Do not add a charting/BI dependency — render with the existing design system
Do not query in the LiveView — read from Kanban.Agents
Do not regress the refresh debounce/presence behavior

key_files.note: May host or sit beside the PM trends section
Assign trend aggregates and render the section

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass including the PM trends section

verification_steps.step_text: Open /agents at localhost:4000 and confirm the PM section shows throughput, success rate, and cycle time and refreshes live, in both light and dark mode
verification_steps.expected_result: Trends visible and correct, live-updating, readable in both modes

Time-series renders for a sample window

goals.title: Add a developer per-agent drill-down to /agents
goals.why: Developers want to inspect a single agent's activity, claims, failures, and current work, but the page offers no detail view. The selected-agent filter already exists in AgentsLive (select_agent / clear_agent_filter); this goal turns that selection into a real drill-down for the developer persona.
goals.what: Add a context function returning a detailed view of one agent (current work, claims, failures, recent activity) derived from existing Task data, and a detail panel on /agents that opens for the currently selected agent within the existing LiveView and design system. Read-only; scope-respecting; both modes.
goals.description: Build on the existing selected_agent state to show a per-agent detail panel: current task, claim history, failures/abandoned work, and recent activity for that one agent. All derived read-only from Task data; respects board scope; works in light and dark mode. Not transcripts or token-level detail (non-goal); not agent control (non-goal).
goals.acceptance_criteria:
A detail view shows a single agent's current work, claims, failures, and recent activity
The detail opens for the agent selected via the existing select_agent flow and closes via clear_agent_filter
All detail data is derived read-only from existing Task fields; no schema change
Respects board scope and the existing live refresh
Readable in light and dark mode
No conversation logs / token detail; no start/stop/reassign controls
pitfalls: Do not add transcript or token-level detail — non-goal
Do not add agent control actions — view-only
Do not query in the LiveView — keep the detail derivation in Kanban.Agents
Do not break the existing select_agent / clear_agent_filter behavior

tasks.title: Add agent_detail derivation to Kanban.Agents
tasks.description: Add a scoped context function Kanban.Agents.agent_detail(name, opts) returning a single agent's current task, claim history, failures/abandoned work, and recent activity, derived from existing Task fields. Returns nil/empty gracefully for an unknown agent. Respects :scope.
tasks.why: The detail panel needs one trustworthy, scope-aware data source; this is the data layer for the drill-down.
tasks.what: Add agent_detail/2 deriving per-agent detail from the visible Task set.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
agent_detail returns current task, claims, failures, and recent activity for the named agent
Derived only from existing Task fields; no schema change
Respects :scope and returns gracefully for an unknown/empty agent
Unit tests cover a populated agent and an unknown agent
tasks.patterns_to_follow: Follow the opts/:scope, fetch_tasks, and Event-derivation patterns already in agents.ex
pitfalls: Avoid N+1 — derive from a single scoped task fetch
Do not leak tasks outside the scoped board set

verification_steps.expected_result: All tests pass including agent_detail cases

Uses existing design tokens; readable in light and dark mode; text gettext-wrapped
Degrades gracefully for sparse data (e.g. no current task)

Run mix gettext.extract --merge after adding text

Do not add control buttons (start/stop/reassign) — view-only
Do not hardcode colors

verification_steps.expected_result: Panel renders all sections cleanly with no hardcoded-color issues in either mode

tasks.title: Wire the detail panel into AgentsLive for the selected agent
tasks.description: In AgentsLive, when an agent is selected (existing select_agent flow), load agent_detail for that agent and render the detail-panel component; clear it on clear_agent_filter. Refresh the detail on the existing PubSub debounce while a selection is active. Respect board scope.
tasks.why: This connects the existing selection state to the new data and component, delivering the developer drill-down end to end.
tasks.what: Assign agent_detail on selection, render the panel, and refresh it on live updates.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
Selecting an agent opens the detail panel populated via Kanban.Agents.agent_detail
clear_agent_filter closes the panel
Detail refreshes on the existing PubSub debounce while a selection is active
Querying stays in the context; LiveView only assigns
Works in light and dark mode; board scope respected
tasks.patterns_to_follow:
Reuse the existing select_agent / clear_agent_filter handlers and selected_agent assign
Follow the load_agents_data assign pattern and refresh debounce
pitfalls: Do not put the agent_detail query in the LiveView — call the context
Do not break the existing event filtering tied to selected_agent
Do not redrive detail on every event without the existing debounce
